)MzjAbstract Base Classes (ABCs) for collections, according to PEP 3119.
Unit tests are in test_collections.

Collection.__subclasshook__N)
_CallableGenericAliasz
 Represent `Callable[argtypes, resulttype]`.
    This sets ``__args__`` to a tuple containing the flattened``argtypes``
    followed by ``resulttype``.
    Example: ``Callable[[int, str], float]`` sets ``__args__`` to
    ``(int, str, float)``.
    r
z. (This will raise a TypeError in Python 3.10.))
 _CallableGenericAlias__create_ga
	TypeError

__classcell__r
Return the repr() of an object, special-casing types (internal helper).
    Copied from :mod:`typing` since collections.abc
    shouldn't depend on that module.
    
builtins
...)	rk
repr
typer8

__call__
Callable.__call__c
Callable.__subclasshook__N)
A set is a finite, iterable container.
    This class provides concrete generic implementations of all
    methods except for __contains__, __iter__ and __len__.
    To override the comparisons (presumably for speed, as the
    semantics are fixed), redefine __le__ and __ge__,
    then the other operations will automatically follow suit.
    r
other
elemr
__le__
Set.__le__c
__lt__
Set.__lt__c
__ge__r
__gt__!
Set.__gt__c
Set.__ge__c
__eq__0
Set.__eq__c
Construct an instance of the class from any iterable input.
        Must override this method if the class constructor signature
        does not accept an iterable for an input.
        r
_from_iterable5
Set._from_iterablec
	<genexpr>A
Set.__and__.<locals>.<genexpr>
__and__>
Set.__and__c
z1Return True if two sets have a null intersection.FTr
isdisjointE
Set.isdisjointc
Set.__or__.<locals>.<genexpr>r
chainr
__or__L

Set.__sub__.<locals>.<genexpr>
__sub__T
Set.__sub__c
Set.__rsub__.<locals>.<genexpr>r
__rsub__\
Set.__rsub__c
__xor__d
Set.__xor__c
Compute the hash value of a set.
        Note that we don't define __hash__: not all sets are hashable.
        But if you define a hashable set type, its __hash__ should
        call this function.
        This must be compatible __eq__.
        All sets ought to compare equal if they contain the same
        elements, regardless of how they are implemented, and
        regardless of the order of the elements; so there's not much
        freedom for __eq__ or __hash__.  We match the algorithm used
        by the built-in frozenset type.
        rj
maxsizerm
hash)
MASK
_hashm
z	Set._hashN)
__rand__r
__ror__r
__rxor__r
A mutable set is a finite, iterable container.
    This class provides concrete generic implementations of all
    methods except for __contains__, __iter__, __len__,
    add(), and discard().
    To override the comparisons (presumably for speed, as the
    semantics are fixed), all you have to do is redefine __le__ and
    then the other operations will automatically follow suit.
    r
Add an element.N
NotImplementedErrorrB
MutableSet.addc
z8Remove an element.  Do not raise an exception if absent.Nr
discard
MutableSet.discardc
z5Remove an element. If not a member, raise a KeyError.N)

remove
MutableSet.removec
z2Return the popped value.  Raise KeyError if empty.N)

MutableMapping.__delitem__c
D.pop(k[,d]) -> v, remove specified key and return the corresponding value.
          If key is not found, d is returned if given, otherwise KeyError is raised.
        N)
_MutableMapping__marker)
MutableMapping.popc
D.popitem() -> (k, v), remove and return some (key, value) pair
           as a 2-tuple; but raise KeyError if D is empty.
        N)
popitem
MutableMapping.popitemc
z,D.clear() -> None.  Remove all items from D.N)
MutableMapping.clearc
 D.update([E, ]**F) -> None.  Update D from mapping/iterable E and F.
            If E present and has a .keys() method, does:     for k in E: D[k] = E[k]
            If E present and lacks .keys() method, does:     for (k, v) in E: D[k] = v
            In either case, this is followed by: for k, v in F.items(): D[k] = v
        r
hasattrr
update
MutableMapping.updateNc
z@D.setdefault(k[,d]) -> D.get(k,d), also set D[k]=d if k not in Dr
setdefault
MutableMapping.setdefault)
objectr
All the operations on a read-only sequence.
    Concrete subclasses must override __new__ or __init__,
    __getitem__, and __len__.
    r

rangerm

S.index(value, [start, [stop]]) -> integer -- return first index of value.
           Raises ValueError if the value is not present.
           Supporting start and stop arguments is optional, but
           recommended.
        Nr
